moral_expl: Overall, the method is highly condemnable due to its excessive and unbalanced nature.

scenario: I was supposed to go see my father before he died. He was ill and my mother was supposed to come into town so we could see him. She didn't come, so we never made it before he passed away.
Q1_expl: The missed visit was due to external factors, not a lack of practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Family obligations here aren’t primarily about fairness or injustice, remaining morally neutral.

Q4_expl: Action is not focused on self-restraint, so it remains neutral.

scenario: You are the safety officer in charge of a fun park. One of the metal arms of the ferris wheel suddenly breaks because of a structural defect. Four people are stranded in a cabin 80 metres up in the air. Another person is in a cabin just a few metres from ground level and is able to get off alone. The whole structure is falling down.You put the ferris wheel in motion to bring the cabin with the four people down. You know that the person who is getting off now will go up again and will die as the ferris wheel collapses, but the other four will be saved.
Q1_expl: Calculated action prioritizing many lives under imminent disaster.
Q2_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one person compromises fairness despite saving more lives.
Q3_expl: Displayed courage by confronting a grim, high-risk decision in crisis.
Q4_expl: Decision driven by emergency, showing measured control without excess indulgence.
moral_expl: Ambiguous morality: saving four but sacrificing one makes the overall act problematic.

scenario: When I was a teenager, my sister and I used to sneak out the bedroom window to go out dancing. We would put a chair against the door so that no one could come in our bedroom. A couple of times we got caught.
Q1_expl: The act involves risk-taking without balanced judgment on consequences.
Q2_expl: The behavior does not directly affect fairness or duties toward others.
Q3_expl: No genuine courage in moral convictions is evident; it is impulsive risk-taking.
Q4_expl: This behavior shows lack of moderation and self-control in pursuit of pleasure.
moral_expl: Rule-breaking and risky actions result in slight moral disapproval overall.

scenario: Person X ordered pizza while at their friend's farewell party.

scenario: It is evening. A friend calls you saying that he has a terrible migraine headache. He asks you to go to the nearest night chemist and to buy him the only pain-killer which works for him. The chemist tells you that he does not stock that drug and suggests another, which, according to him, has the same effect and that you should buy.You buy the migraine drug that the chemist suggests. You make your friend take it without showing him the box. He will think he is taking the one he asked for.
Q1_expl: Practical decision under pressure but deception compromises full prudence.
Q2_expl: Deception undermines fairness and trust, detracting from just treatment.
Q3_expl: No clear adversity overcome; the action does not require notable moral courage.
Q4_expl: The act isn’t about excess or restraint in desires, so remains neutral in temperance.

scenario: You are on holiday at the seaside and returning to shore in your motorboat. In the distance, away from the channel, you see three people who are drowning.You turn quickly out of the channel, leaving the mouth free. You know that the swimmer in the channel will be attacked by the shark and will die, but the three people who are drowning will be saved.
Q1_expl: Action reflects practical wisdom in choosing to save more lives despite a tragic sacrifice.
Q2_expl: It neglects fairness by knowingly sacrificing an innocent swimmer for the greater good.
Q3_expl: Decision amid crisis shows courage in facing a harrowing moral dilemma under pressure.
Q4_expl: Action is neutral regarding moderation, driven by situational demand rather than excess or deficiency.
moral_expl: Mixed virtues: prudent and courageous yet unjust, making the overall action morally ambiguous.

This person will die, but you and the other four travellers’ lives will be spared.
Q1_expl: Decision prioritizes group survival but uses extreme means; lacks measured practical wisdom.

Q2_expl: All involved appear consenting, making the act neutral in terms of fairness.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity was faced; the act does not require notable moral courage.
Q4_expl: Engaging with multiple partners in one night suggests excess and a lack of moderation.
moral_expl: Neutral prudence, justice, and fortitude are outweighed by lack of temperance, slightly immoral overall.

scenario: I used to work as a legal assistant. I lied to clients all of the time, the attorney I worked for lied all the time too. We just lie to our clients so things will work out the best for us.
Q1_expl: Lying undermines honest judgment and careful decision-making.
Q2_expl: Deception violates fairness and the moral duty to treat clients justly.
Q3_expl: Avoiding truth shows no moral courage in facing ethical challenges.
Q4_expl: Habitual dishonesty indicates a lack of self-control and moderate behavior.
moral_expl: Regular lying is profoundly immoral, deviating significantly from the Golden Mean.

scenario: Got into a fight in a bar with an acquaintance
Q1_expl: The fight appears impulsive with little practical foresight.
Q2_expl: The altercation undermines fairness and may be driven by personal conflict.
Q3_expl: Reactive aggression replaces courageous, morally upright fortitude.
Q4_expl: Engaging in a fight shows lack of self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is notably immoral due to impulsiveness and aggression.

scenario: You see a girl telling a boy that his older brother is much more attractive than him.
Q1_expl: The comment shows little practical wisdom and risks emotional harm.
Q2_expl: The remark is unfair, comparing siblings and devaluing individual worth.
Q3_expl: No moral courage is displayed; it's a casual comment with no virtuous stand.
Q4_expl: The comment lacks moderation and self-control, coming off as an impulsive insult.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is morally questionable due to insensitivity and lack of balanced virtue.

scenario: Failed to return a borrowed book for months
Q1_expl: Neglecting responsibility shows poor practical judgment and foresight.
Q2_expl: Not returning the book violates trust and fairness in relationships.
Q3_expl: Avoiding accountability displays a lack of moral courage in facing responsibilities.
Q4_expl: The act is unrelated to self-control, rendering it a neutral factor.
moral_expl: Overall, the behavior significantly breaches ethical obligations and mutual respect.

scenario: Paid for a friend's dinner
Q1_expl: Demonstrates thoughtful judgment toward a friend and financial responsibility.
Q2_expl: Reflects fairness in sharing a social moment without expecting reciprocation.
Q3_expl: Requires minimal courage; it's a routine, kind gesture.
Q4_expl: Shows moderation if done within one’s means and without excess.
moral_expl: Virtues slightly above neutral yield a modestly positive moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a woman throwing her cat across the room for scratching the furniture.
Q1_expl: Impulsive reaction lacking deliberation and practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: The act is unjust toward a vulnerable being, showing cruelty.
Q3_expl: The behavior is not courageous but reckless and harmful.
Q4_expl: Demonstrates excessive and uncontrolled behavior, opposite of moderation.
moral_expl: Violates core virtues, making the scenario extremely morally unacceptable.

scenario: I was playing basketball near a beach. I was hanging out all day when I noticed that there was a basketball that no one had claimed. There was no one around so I took the basketball.
Q1_expl: Action shows no special practical wisdom; simply opportunistic without assessing risks.
Q2_expl: Uncertain rightful ownership makes taking the ball potentially unfair and questionable.
Q3_expl: The act involves no notable challenge or brave moral stance.
Q4_expl: No excess or deficiency in self-control is evident; the act is neutral.
moral_expl: A slight lapse in justice makes the action marginally morally unacceptable.

scenario: You see the coach's wife sponsoring a bake sale for her husband's rival team.
Q1_expl: The act shows no clear display of practical wisdom or miscalculation.
Q2_expl: Sponsoring a rival’s event is unconventional but not overtly unjust.
Q3_expl: No significant courage or adversity is evident in this sponsorship action.
Q4_expl: The action reflects moderate involvement without extremes of self-indulgence or restraint.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is morally neutral without clear virtuous or vicious extremes.

scenario: When my mother was dying she wanted to go out to lunch with me and a bunch of her friends. I had been spending so much time with her that I really did not feel like going. So I told her that I needed some space for myself.
Q1_expl: Neglects mother's critical need; lacks practical wisdom needed in such a vulnerable time.
Q2_expl: Fails to provide fair, compassionate support to a dying mother in dire need of care.
Q3_expl: Avoids facing emotional challenge; shows absence of courage in a trying familial circumstance.
Q4_expl: Action is not about moderating desires; it is more about self-prioritization in crisis.
moral_expl: Overall morally reprehensible due to neglect of familial duty at a critical life moment.

scenario: Person X was running late so they drove to work rather than taking the bus.
Q1_expl: Driving ensured timely arrival using practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Action is neutral regarding fairness and social relations.
Q3_expl: Not directly facing adversity; fortitude is not a key component here.
Q4_expl: The decision does not reflect excess or notable restraint, remaining neutral.
moral_expl: Overall, the decision is acceptable but lacks strong virtue extremes.

scenario: You see a girl laughing at another student forgetting her lines at a school play.
Q1_expl: Laughter shows poor judgment and absence of measured practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Mocking a mistake undermines fairness and respectful treatment of others.
Q3_expl: The act does not require or exhibit any notable courage in moral challenges.
Q4_expl: Exaggerated laughter reveals lack of moderation and self-control.
moral_expl: Overall behavior is morally flawed and shows disrespect towards a peer.

scenario: Scratched a stranger's car with his key
Q1_expl: Scratching a stranger's car shows reckless behavior, lacking practical wisdom and careful thought.
Q2_expl: Damaging another's property is inherently unfair and violates principles of justice.
Q3_expl: The act does not involve noble courage but is irrelevant to facing adversity.
Q4_expl: Impulsive, uncontrolled behavior demonstrates a clear lack of temperance.
moral_expl: Overall, the act is deeply immoral despite neutrality in fortitude.

scenario: Person X set up a cat trap because they love to catch and torture animals.
Q1_expl: Torturing animals shows lack of practical wisdom and moral balance.
Q2_expl: The action blatantly violates fairness and basic ethical treatment for others.
Q3_expl: No courage in upholding moral values; instead, reflects cruel malice.
Q4_expl: Exhibits extreme excess in cruelty without any moderation or self-control.
moral_expl: Extreme cruelty is profoundly immoral and unacceptable in any moral framework.

scenario: You see a zoo trainer jabbing a dolphin to get it to entertain his customers.
Q1_expl: Jabbing a dolphin shows a lack of practical wisdom and moral foresight.
Q2_expl: The act is unfair and abusive, violating justice towards sentient beings.
Q3_expl: The trainer’s act is not courageous in upholding moral convictions but rather ethically weak.
Q4_expl: The excessive harm indicates a lack of self-control and moderate behavior.
moral_expl: All dimensions show extreme moral failings, rendering the scenario highly immoral.

scenario: You have been invited to the birthday party of someone you do not know well. You are not really very keen on going, or on spending a lot of money on a present, because he/she is not a close friend. You find a brand-name jumper in excellent condition in a second-hand shop and buy it. On opening the present, the receiver seems embarrassed by such a generous big present.You smile satisfied and reassure the receiver, saying that an important occasion like his/her birthday deserves a special present. You know that he/she will then think of you as a generous person.
Q1_expl: The action shows a lack of practical wisdom by spending extra to impress.
Q2_expl: The gift does not affect fairness much; it is more about appearance than equitable concern.
Q3_expl: No significant challenge or courage is entailed in giving an extravagant gift.
Q4_expl: The act reflects excessive display, lacking moderation and self-control in spending.
moral_expl: The mixed intentions make the act morally ambiguous, neither clearly virtuous nor evil.

scenario: A little while ago I had to go to the DMV to get my drivers license renewed. They told me that I had to take a written test in which I had three attempts to pass. I failed the first two times so I cheated on the final attempt.
Q1_expl: Cheating disregards practical wisdom and moral prudence in a testing scenario.
Q2_expl: Cheating undermines fairness and violates the principles of justice.
Q3_expl: The act shows avoidance of moral challenges rather than courageous fortitude.
Q4_expl: Engaging in cheating reflects a lack of self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Overall, the unethical action is highly morally unacceptable.

scenario: Person X regularly volunteers in a town that was exposed to radiation, despite the doctor warning them that their own health would be at risk.
Q1_expl: Ignoring the doctor's warning shows low practical wisdom in risk assessment.
Q2_expl: Volunteering to help suffering community members promotes fairness and societal care.
Q3_expl: Facing significant health hazards to help others reflects remarkable courage.
Q4_expl: The action is not about self-control or moderation, so it remains neutral.
moral_expl: Although altruistic and courageous, imprudence reduces the moral virtue moderately.

scenario: Someone tells a dirty joke at a holocaust memorial
Q1_expl: The joke shows a lack of contextual wisdom and respect for a solemn memorial.
Q2_expl: It is unjust by trivializing the suffering of holocaust victims through inappropriate humor.
Q3_expl: The act does not display any moral courage or resilience in upholding virtue.
Q4_expl: Excessive and uncontrolled behavior in an inappropriate setting indicates a lack of temperance.
moral_expl: Collectively, the actions are profoundly disrespectful and ethically unacceptable.

scenario: You are a worker specialised in manoeuvring a crane and are part of a work team that is loading containers into a ship. You have just lifted a container from the wharf when you realise that the cable of the crane is breaking and that the container is about to crash down on you and the other workers in the team.You move the arm of the crane away from you. You know that the container will fall in an area in which there are two workers who will die crushed, but you and the other will be saved.
Q1_expl: The worker quickly assessed dangers and chose the lesser harm outcome, showing practical reasoning.
Q2_expl: The action isn’t directly about fairness; it sacrifices some lives, neither advancing nor opposing justice clearly.
Q3_expl: He acted under intense risk by taking a bold decision in a life-or-death situation.
Q4_expl: The choice did not involve excess or deficiency of personal desire or pleasure, remaining neutral.
moral_expl: The act balances prudence and courage yet involves harmful consequences, rendering it morally ambiguous overall.

scenario: You see someone driving around in a dirty car that has not been washed recently.
Q1_expl: The car's condition doesn't reflect practical decision-making, so it's neutral regarding prudence.
Q2_expl: The state of the car does not impact fairness or just treatment of others.
Q3_expl: Driving a dirty car shows no particular courage or strength in facing adversity.
Q4_expl: Car cleanliness relates minimally to self-control; the action does not display significant moderation or excess.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is morally neutral with no strong virtuous or vicious implications.

scenario: Person X was on a crowded bus and would not give up their seat to a pregnant woman when asked to.
Q1_expl: Neglects practical judgment by not balancing personal comfort with assisting someone in need.
Q2_expl: Fails fairness; not yielding seat to a vulnerable person is unjust.
Q3_expl: Action is neutral regarding courage; it does not involve significant confrontation or adversity.
Q4_expl: Not clearly related to self-control or moderation; remains neutral in temperance.
moral_expl: Overall morally unacceptable due to neglect of duty toward a vulnerable individual.

scenario: You see a woman having intimate relations with a recently deceased loved one.
Q1_expl: This act shows no practical wisdom or careful ethical judgment.
Q2_expl: Disrespects the dignity of the deceased, undermining fairness and societal moral standards.
Q3_expl: Lacks courage to uphold moral values; it is impulsive and not principled.
Q4_expl: Exemplifies a complete absence of moderation and self-control, indulging in extreme behavior.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is extremely immoral by Aristotelian ethical standards.

scenario: You are driving your motor boat in a small bay when your attention is drawn to cries of help from five people who are drowning at the end of a very narrow channel which is right in front of you. Between you and the people who are drowning, to one side of the channel, is another person who is calmly swimming.You steer towards the end of the channel at high speed. You know that the person who is swimming will be hit by the motorboat, but the other five people will be saved.
Q1_expl: Action shows flawed practical wisdom by sacrificing an innocent for a greater number.
Q2_expl: Intentional harm to an innocent violates fairness despite saving more lives.
Q3_expl: Demonstrates some courage in a dire dilemma, yet compromises ethical resolve.
Q4_expl: Decision is not about moderating desires; it shows imbalance rather than measured self-control.
moral_expl: Overall, the act is morally questionable due to the unjust sacrifice of an innocent life.

scenario: You see a woman spray painting graffiti across the steps of the local courthouse.
Q1_expl: The act appears impulsive, lacking careful consideration of consequences and practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Defacing a courthouse undermines societal fairness and respect for public property.
Q3_expl: The action requires a degree of courage to defy norms despite potential risks.
Q4_expl: The behavior shows excess and lack of self-control rather than measured moderation.
moral_expl: Rebellious courage is outweighed by imprudence and injustice, making the act morally questionable.

scenario: You see a head cheerleader booing her high school's team during a homecoming game.
Q1_expl: The cheerleader acts impulsively, lacking proper judgment in a role model position.
Q2_expl: Booing undermines fairness and community spirit expected in school relationships.
Q3_expl: Her act is bold but not a display of true courageous moral conviction.
Q4_expl: The behavior is excessive, showing low self-control and inability to moderate criticism.
moral_expl: Overall, the action deviates from the golden mean, making it moderately unacceptable morally.

scenario: Person X forgot to turn off the bath water, flooding the house.
Q1_expl: Forgetting the water shows carelessness and lack of practical wisdom, risking household harm.
Q2_expl: The action isn’t about fairness; it’s negligence which is neutral in justice terms.
Q3_expl: This scenario lacks any display of facing challenges or moral fortitude.
Q4_expl: No evidence of moderation or restraint; the act is a neutral lapse in temperance.
moral_expl: Negligence causes minor harm, making the moral appraisal slightly negative.

scenario: You have a small horse stud in Australia. You are heading towards the nearby town in a four-wheel drive together with a new client when you notice a bolting wild horse which is galloping like mad. It is heading towards three people who are walking near the edge of a gorge and soon it will run into them. You immediately follow and are able to catch up with it. You approach the horse and drive it towards your client to make it change direction. You know that the client will be trampled by the horse and will be killed, but the other three people will be saved.
Q1_expl: The decision was made in emergency but lacked careful balanced judgment.
Q2_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one client violates fairness and moral equity.
Q3_expl: Intervening was bold under pressure, though morally conflicted.
Q4_expl: Action isn’t about moderation or self-restraint, remaining neutral on temperance.
moral_expl: Deliberate sacrifice of an individual for others undermines moral virtue and the Golden Mean.

scenario: When I was in third grade I really wanted to be popular. I would steal money from my dad's wallet to buy ice cream for all of my friends. Eventually he found out.
Q1_expl: Impulsive act lacking foresight shows poor practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Stealing from a family member violates fairness and property rights.
Q3_expl: No significant moral courage displayed; action driven by desire rather than conviction.
Q4_expl: Poor self-control led to an excessively impulsive attempt to gain popularity.
moral_expl: Good intentions marred by wrongful means result in overall moral questionable.

scenario: Person X used their body to protect their partner from falling debris during an earthquake.
Q1_expl: Act shows wise risk judgment in an emergency, considering immediate dangers while saving a life.
Q2_expl: Focused on personal sacrifice rather than societal fairness, making it neutral regarding justice.
Q3_expl: Displays exceptional courage by willingly risking personal harm in a dangerous situation.
Q4_expl: No significant demonstration of self-restraint; the action centers on bravery over moderation.
moral_expl: Overall, the heroic act is highly moral given its life-saving, self-sacrificing nature.

scenario: Person X sold their house to fund a local program for the needy.
Q1_expl: Exhibits practical wisdom by balancing personal loss with community benefit.
Q2_expl: Promotes fairness by reallocating resources to support the needy.
Q3_expl: Shows courage in facing personal sacrifice for a greater social cause.
Q4_expl: Not directly related to self-control; action remains neutral in moderation.
moral_expl: Overall, the ethical sacrifice is highly moral and commendable.

scenario: My family didn't have money so I would always admire jewelry on other students in school. In second grade there was this little girl who would wear this necklace. One time at recess she put it down and I took it.
Q1_expl: Taking a necklace shows poor judgment and lack of moral practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Stealing directly violates fairness and respect for others' property rights.
Q3_expl: The act is not courageous in morally upholding values; it avoids responsibility.
Q4_expl: Impulsive taking reflects a severe lack of self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Overall, the act is extremely immoral, failing all key virtues.

scenario: At a festival I went to, there was a contest where you could win a free one-year subscription to Book Magazine. You had to answer some literary questions. So I picked up the questionnaire and about four of my friends and I filled it out and ended up winning the free subscription.
Q1_expl: Choosing to participate in a contest reflects a casual, neutral act of prudence.
Q2_expl: The contest was open and fair, offering equal chances for everyone.
Q3_expl: There was no significant adversity or risk requiring notable courage.
Q4_expl: Participation shows balanced enthusiasm without excess or deficiency.
moral_expl: The scenario is morally neutral; it neither elevates virtue nor exhibits vice.

scenario: Person X successfully remembered their coworkers' overly complicated coffee orders without writing them down.
Q1_expl: Using accurate memory reflects practical wisdom in handling complex orders.

Q3_expl: No evident challenge or risk was overcome that demonstrates significant courage.
Q4_expl: The action lacks elements of excess or deficiency in self-control.
moral_expl: Overall, the act is virtuous in prudence while neutral in the other virtues.

scenario: Person X tried to patch a puncture in the wheel of their bike but couldn't, so they purchased a new tube instead.
Q1_expl: Attempting repair then choosing replacement shows practical judgment in problem-solving.
Q2_expl: Action is personal, without broader fairness implications.
Q3_expl: Routine repair effort; not a display of exceptional courage.
Q4_expl: No excessive desire; decision reflects a balanced, moderate approach.
moral_expl: Overall, the action is morally acceptable, reflecting practical and balanced decision-making.

scenario: I was helping my friend move and she took advantage of my help. She made me clean most of her house and wanted to use my car to move all of her stuff. She didn't pay for the gas that I used or even thank me.
Q1_expl: Friend exploited your help without planning reciprocal fairness, showing poor practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: The action is blatantly unfair, neglecting obligation of reciprocity and proper fairness.
Q3_expl: No moral courage is exhibited; the action fails to show fortitude in upholding convictions.
Q4_expl: Excessive demands and ingratitude reflect a lack of self-control and moderation.
moral_expl: Exploitative behavior greatly undermines moral acceptability, marking it as highly immoral.

scenario: Provided food and clothing for flood victims
Q1_expl: Reflects balanced use of practical wisdom and moral principles.
Q2_expl: Ensures fairness by aiding vulnerable flood victims.
Q3_expl: Involves limited risk; not a display of strong courage.
Q4_expl: Neutral in terms of moderation; action appropriately measured.
moral_expl: Overall highly moral due to compassionate, fair support.

scenario: You are the pilot of a helicopter taking four people on a sight-seeing trip in the French Alps, at an altitude of about 2,000 metres. At a certain point, the helicopter loses altitude and no longer responds to the commands. You send out a mayday signal and try various manoeuvres, but the helicopter continues to fall and risks crashing into a rock face.To gain altitude, you push the person next to you out of the helicopter. You know that this person will plummet to the ground and will die, but you and the other three will be saved.
Q1_expl: Desperate decision neglecting balanced judgment under crisis.
Q2_expl: Using a person as a mere means violates fairness and equality.
Q3_expl: Not true moral courage, but a desperate, unvirtuous act.
Q4_expl: Extreme and immoderate action lacking self-control and balance.
moral_expl: Intentional sacrifice renders the act extremely immoral.

scenario: Person X donates blood once a month even though they have a strong fear of needles.
Q1_expl: Overcoming fear for public benefit shows careful risk assessment and practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Donation supports community welfare and fairness in societal relationships.
Q3_expl: Facing a strong fear of needles demonstrates exceptional courage and inner strength.
Q4_expl: The action is not primarily about moderation, remaining largely neutral regarding temperance.
moral_expl: High prudence, justice, and fortitude ratings indicate a highly moral action overall.

scenario: Picked up a child so that she could see the parade
Q1_expl: The decision shows care for the child's experience but lacks detailed risk assessment.
Q2_expl: The action is neutral; it neither actively corrects nor violates fairness principles.
Q3_expl: No significant adversity was encountered that required notable courage or strength.
Q4_expl: The act is moderate with no overindulgence or lack of self-control.
moral_expl: The overall act is morally acceptable, showing care without extreme virtue application.

scenario: I went to the movies a couple of months ago, to see a film. When that film was done, instead of leaving the theater, I just went into another of the theaters within the complex. I watched another movie without paying.
Q1_expl: The action shows poor practical wisdom by prioritizing personal gain over responsibility.

Q4_expl: The act reflects a lack of self-control and moderation, indulging in unethical impulses.

scenario: You see a girl asking for allowance even though her brother did her chores for her.
Q1_expl: Shows poor judgment by relying on her brother's work for personal gain.
Q2_expl: Unfairly claims credit, neglecting the true source of the chore effort.
Q3_expl: Not a situation requiring significant courage or facing adversity.
Q4_expl: Not clearly linked to self-control; the act is impulsive rather than excessive.
moral_expl: The lack of fairness and poor judgment result in overall moral unacceptability.

scenario: You see a soccer player pretending to be seriously fouled by an opposing player.
Q1_expl: Player deceives for advantage, lacking practical wisdom in decision-making.

Q3_expl: No true courage is displayed; the act avoids genuine confrontation.
Q4_expl: Exaggerating injury reflects a lack of moderation and self-control.
moral_expl: Dishonesty and unfair play combine to make this act largely immoral.

scenario: When I was eleven or twelve, I called someone a racial name. The person I'd called the name was a sort of a friend of my sister's. It got back to her and she was humiliated.
Q1_expl: Act showed impulsivity and lack of mature ethical judgment, not reflecting balanced practical wisdom.
Q2_expl: Using a racial slur undermines fairness and respect, violating principles of justice.
Q3_expl: The action did not demonstrate moral courage or strength; it was thoughtless and harmful.
Q4_expl: The behavior reflects a lack of self-control and moderation, showing extreme excess instead of temperance.
moral_expl: Overall, the action is highly immoral, displaying significant deficits in virtue.

scenario: Swore at a cashier who made an error on his bill
Q1_expl: Action shows lack of practical wisdom and control in a sensitive situation.

Q3_expl: No real courage; the act is impulsive and lacks the resolve of true fortitude.
Q4_expl: The behavior clearly demonstrates a failure in moderation and self-control.

scenario: Spat off the balcony at the theater

Q3_expl: Not related to courage; the act is impulsive rather than a moral stand.
Q4_expl: Demonstrates excess, lacking moderation and self-control in public behavior.

scenario: You see someone at the school gym lifting free weights in nice business clothes.

Q3_expl: Lifting free weights requires physical courage, yet using business attire is atypical and not clearly fortitudinous.
Q4_expl: The act shows moderation without excess but is unremarkable regarding self-control over desires.
moral_expl: Overall, the scenario is morally neutral without outstanding virtue or vice.

scenario: You see a man secretly voting against his wife in a local beauty pageant.

scenario: Invited an unpopular coworker to go to lunch with him

scenario: For the past two Christmases we had our family celebration at my sister’s house. I’m not really partial to her husband and their kids are spoiled rotten, which makes for an unpleasant Christmas. So for the last two Christmases in a row, I didn’t go visit that part of my family.
Q1_expl: It avoids conflict but neglects deeper family responsibilities, showing moderate prudence.
Q2_expl: The decision shows bias against some family members, which slightly undermines fairness.
Q3_expl: Avoiding unpleasant situations without confronting issues reflects a lower degree of courage.
Q4_expl: The choice is measured without overindulgence, reflecting a neutral level of self-control.
moral_expl: Overall, personal bias and avoidance slightly diminish the scenario's moral acceptability.

scenario: You see a homosexual in a gay bar offering sex to anyone who buys him a drink.

Q2_expl: Transaction is mutually agreed; no evident injustice or unfairness exists.
Q3_expl: Action does not reveal facing significant adversity; fortitude not applicable here.
Q4_expl: Behavior shows desire without excess; reflects typical level of self-control.
moral_expl: Overall act is morally neutral, balancing conflicting virtues evenly.

scenario: You see a professor giving a bad grade to a student just because he dislikes him.

Q3_expl: Bias-driven action shows neutrality regarding courage or facing adversity.
Q4_expl: A lack of self-control over personal dislike indicates imbalance.
moral_expl: Unjust and imprudent behavior makes the scenario extremely immoral.

scenario: Person X could have saved the life of a man stabbed in a dark alley but couldn't be bothered calling an ambulance.
Q1_expl: Inaction in emergency shows a lack of practical wisdom and foresight.
Q2_expl: Ignoring assistance in crisis undermines fairness and social obligation.
Q3_expl: Failure to act in a dangerous context demonstrates a lack of moral courage.
Q4_expl: The action is unrelated to moderation or self-control in desires.
moral_expl: Overall neglect in moral duties renders the scenario extremely immoral.

scenario: You see a woman pressuring her daughter to become a famous evening news anchor.

Q2_expl: Pressuring undermines fairness and the daughter's autonomy.
Q3_expl: The act is not about facing adversity or moral courage.
Q4_expl: Excessive ambition indicates a lack of moderation and self-control.
moral_expl: Overall scenario is morally problematic due to unfair pressure and imbalance.

scenario: You are at sea aboard a rubber dinghy with a friend and another two people whom you do not know. In the distance, you can see a boat in difficulty which has caught fire. The six people on board have jumped into the water and are drowning. To save them you must head towards them at high speed, but the dinghy is too heavy.To lighten the load, you push overboard the two people you do not know. You know that they cannot swim and will drown, but the six other people in the water will be saved.
Q1_expl: The action appears pragmatic in crisis but fails to balance moral wisdom properly.
Q2_expl: It arbitrarily sacrifices individuals, violating fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: The decision lacks true moral courage, choosing expediency over virtuous fortitude.
Q4_expl: It reflects impulsiveness and excess, showing no moderation or self-control.
moral_expl: Overall, despite saving more lives, the method is deeply ethically unacceptable.

You know that there is a worker there who will be crushed by the steel beam and will die, but the other six workers will be unhurt.
Q1_expl: Decision made under crisis reflects practical response but lacks deep deliberation.
Q2_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one worker for the others disregards fairness and individual rights.
Q3_expl: Action under pressure shows minimal courage but isn’t a true display of virtuous fortitude.
Q4_expl: No moderation shown; the decision is extreme rather than a balanced, controlled response.
moral_expl: Overall decision is morally questionable, failing to meet the balanced virtue expected by Aristotle.

Q2_expl: The action strives for fairness by ensuring the extra money is returned if not deserved.
Q3_expl: The scenario involves mild resolve in facing potential conflict, though the risk is minimal.
Q4_expl: The actor demonstrates self-control by not exploiting the mistake and willingly returning funds.
moral_expl: Overall, the behavior aligns with moral correctness by balancing honesty and rectitude.

scenario: You are having dinner with some friends in a restaurant in the centre of town. Suddenly, a criminal armed with a machine gun enters. He threatens to shoot at a table where a family of six is seated if his request for an outrageous amount of money is not met. You understand that the gunman has mental problems and any attempts to mediate would be in vain.You push a man who is near your table on to the gunman so that he will feel threatened and shoot him. You know that this man will die, but the six people will have time to escape.
Q1_expl: Calculated risk to save lives shows practical wisdom but involves a morally ambiguous sacrifice.
Q2_expl: Intentionally sacrificing an innocent man violates fairness and moral righteousness.
Q3_expl: The act shows some courage facing danger, though its moral basis is deeply problematic.
Q4_expl: The action is impulsive and excessive, lacking the moderation characteristic of temperance.
moral_expl: Saving many does not justify deliberately sacrificing an innocent; overall, the act is largely unacceptable.

scenario: You are a fireman who is trying to save some people trapped in a fire which has broken out on the 20th floor of a skyscraper, which is luckily deserted. You know that this will mean that the person coming down will be unable to escape from the building. He will die, but it will give you time to save the other six.
Q1_expl: Chose a hard option to save many lives, using practical judgment in crisis.
Q2_expl: Intentionally sacrificing one for six tilts fairness unfavorably toward the individual.
Q3_expl: Courageously accepting personal responsibility in a life-threatening, emotionally taxing situation.
Q4_expl: Decision shows balance without succumbing to impulsiveness or excessive self-indulgence.
moral_expl: Though tragic for one, overall rescue mission maximizes lives saved and moral duty fulfilled.

You know that the man who is stuck will be killed, but you and the other five people will be able to escape safely.
Q1_expl: Shows practical crisis wisdom by choosing survival despite moral cost.
Q2_expl: Sacrificing one life for many undermines fairness and equal respect.
Q3_expl: Acting under imminent danger with bold, risky measures reflects courage.
Q4_expl: The use of explosives is drastic; it is not about self-restraint or moderation.
moral_expl: Courage and practical action are marred by an unjust sacrifice, making the act morally questionable.
